name: Kate Black, EMILY's List
subject: Our women protecting Obamacare >>
body:
| [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c708a/24c69fd9/fd9c48a3/18ee82b9/1150223684/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQENE110N_digital_email_20170111_ACAoppo_n-all&source=WQENE110N_digital_email_20170111_ACAoppo_n-all")
---

Peter,

Republicans in the House and Senate are rapidly moving forward bills to repeal Obamacare -- but we _still_ don't have any details on what, if anything, they'll replace it with.

Women and families will lose BIG if Republicans carry out their plan.

Insurance companies could deny coverage for pre-existing conditions, charge women more for health care, and make it harder to access birth control. Young adults could be kicked off of their parents' insurance plans. And the more than 20 million people who've gained health insurance thanks to President Obama could lose it.

Democrats in Congress are prepared to fight back, and women like Sen. Elizabeth Warren, Democratic Leader Nancy Pelosi, Sen. Tammy Duckworth, and Sen. Patty Murray are leading the way.

**[If we want to stop these attacks on women, families, and health care then we have to replace Republicans in Congress. Donate $3 to elect more leaders like Warren, Pelosi, Duckworth, and Murray who will always fight for us.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c708a/24c69fd9/fd9c48a3/18ee82ba/1150223684/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170111_email_ACAoppo?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+If+we+want+to+stop+these+attacks+on+wome&utm_campaign=WQENE110N_digital_email_20170111_ACAoppo_n-all&source=WQENE110N_digital_email_20170111_ACAoppo_n-all")**

Republicans aren't just setting their sights on Obamacare.

Paul Ryan has committed to defunding Planned Parenthood in the House legislation that repeals Obamacare. His plan would create a health care crisis for millions of Americans.

This isn't politics as usual. Republicans are determined to dismantle decades of progress. They have no plans to assist families who will need help if their insurance is taken away and Planned Parenthood clinics are shuttered.

We couldn't ask for better women to fight for us than the dozens of pro-choice Democratic women in Congress. But they need backup.

**It's time to roll up our sleeves and get to work. Donate $3 right now to help elect more pro-choice Democratic women who will fight to protect our health care and Planned Parenthood:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Protect-Obamacare-And-PP](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c708a/24c69fd9/fd9c48a3/18ee82bb/1150223684/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170111_email_ACAoppo?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgProtectObamacare&utm_campaign=WQENE110N_digital_email_20170111_ACAoppo_n-all&source=WQENE110N_digital_email_20170111_ACAoppo_n-all")**

Thanks,

Kate Black
Vice President of Research, EMILY's List

[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c708a/24c69fd9/fd9c48a3/18ee82b4/1150223684/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170111_email_ACAoppo?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQENE110N_digital_email_20170111_ACAoppo_n-all&source=WQENE110N_digital_email_20170111_ACAoppo_n-all")

body:
It’s time to get to work. Help decide our Party’s priorities for 2017. |

Friend, In 7 days, your government will once again work for YOU. Now that the House, the Senate, and the White House will ALL be under Republican control, it’s time for you to help prioritize the policies we’re going to pass in 2017 to Make America Great Again! Inauguration Day is coming up fast, and I want to get to work the second I’m sworn in as your next president. Please complete your 2017 Priorities Survey immediately. >> I want to be sure that I’m starting my presidency fighting for you, and representing the issues you care about -- and that I’m doing it in the order you want. From draining the swamp of corruption to repealing and replacing Obamacare, cutting regulations, reforming our broken tax system, building the wall, and reducing our trade deficit, there’s a lot we need to do to fix our country, but we have to prioritize. I’m heading to Washington to make deals that benefit the American people, that bring back American jobs, that reignite American energy, and that strengthen America as a whole. But I need you to tell me which specific policies you want me to pass as your champion in the White House. Please complete the 2017 Priorities Survey registered in your name. Thank you, Donald J. Trump

name: DNC War Room
subject: re: last night's vote
body:
Last night, the GOP voted to move forward with repealing Obamacare
---
**In the middle of the night, the GOP moved forward with their plan to repeal the Affordable Care Act. Add your name right now to call on Congress to protect health care:

** **[ADD YOUR NAME](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Protect-Health-Care1?keycode=&firstname=\(null\)&lastname=\(null\)&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20170112_dnc2_s2&source=em_20170112_dnc2_s2")**
Friend --

Late last night, at around 1:00 a.m. ET, Senate Republicans voted to move forward with repealing the Affordable Care Act with no plan to replace it. They also blocked Democratic attempts to protect important provisions like ensuring coverage for people with pre-existing conditions and letting young people stay on their parents' insurance.

This is just the first step in the repeal process -- which means there's still time to take action before millions of Americans lose access to health care.

**Can you add your name right now to send Congress a message that you want them to protect health care?**

**[ADD YOUR NAME](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Protect-Health-Care1?keycode=&firstname=\(null\)&lastname=\(null\)&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20170112_dnc2_s2&source=em_20170112_dnc2_s2")**
Thanks,

The Democrats

P.S. -- Once you've signed your name, make sure the GOP keeps hearing from you. **[Click here to use our easy tool to call Republican senators and let them know you want Congress to protect the Affordable Care Act, not repeal it.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c1f7/4067b0a/232cc1424/7e7982ea/4188696012/VEsF/ "http://my.democrats.org/Call-Congress1?campaign=aca&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+Click+here+to+use+our+easy+tool+to+call+&utm_campaign=em_20170112_dnc2_s2&source=em_20170112_dnc2_s2")** It only takes a few minutes, and hearing directly from constituents makes a big difference.

name: Team Kasich
subject: Gov. Kasich's Latest Op-Ed
body:
We wanted to share with you Gov. John Kasichs latest op-ed - a
call for America to strengthen our international alliances, to
stand up to those who seek to divide us and to proudly champion
the values that have paved the way for freedom around the world.

Peter, we wanted to share with
you Gov. John Kasich's latest op-ed - a call for America to
strengthen our international alliances, to stand up to those who
seek to divide us and to proudly champion the values that have
paved the way for freedom around the world.

Excerpt:

"Fast forward to today, and we
can see some of the same erosive forces at work: a weakening NATO
and growing conflict in regions such as Georgia, Crimea and
Eastern Ukraine. Perennial tensions and bloody conflict in the
Middle East are joined by gathering storm clouds in North Korea
and the South China Sea - and overhanging it all, international
terrorism. Calls for disengagement, isolation and shuttered trade
add to the sense that our world is coming undone.

Our nation is safer when freedom,
democracy and the rule of law are embraced around the world. In
places like Ukraine, there is a deep yearning for these values
and we must encourage that desire, not dismiss it. The notion
that it somehow makes us safer at home to sacrifice support for a
free Ukraine in exchange for a better relationship with Russia -
which continues to deny its unacceptable interference in our
presidential election - is wrong and naïve. It's inconsistent
with our ideals and leads other allies to doubt our resolve.
Putin only respects strength, which is one reason why I support
Senator John McCain's call for tougher sanctions against Russia
and Putin's inner circle."

Read more from TIME and please
take a moment to help us share it on Facebook and Twitter.

Full Op-Ed:

It is conventional wisdom that
history repeats itself. But the world should hope - and pray -
that this maxim is off-base when it comes to our global security.
Security arrangements, like the North Atlantic Treaty
Organization (NATO), which through 71 years since the end of
World War II have kept us safe from yet another global conflict,
are quickly showing signs of coming undone. And if history is
indeed repeating itself, we can't say we haven't been warned, and
we must take steps now to change our current course.

As governor of a state that is
restoring its fiscal well-being by keeping pace with the demands
and rewards of a global economy, I am increasingly concerned
about the future of international alliances that has long ensured
the United States and its partner nations of a stable world and
the free flow of ideas and trade. These are not new concerns for
me, having served nine terms in Congress, including 18 years on
the House Armed Services Committee. From those perspectives I
have feared this unraveling for some time, but my concerns are
redoubled by recent threats to our alliances, both here and
abroad.

Growing tensions across the
world, fed by intemperate voices at home, have caused many in
this county to second-guess the international alliances and
relationships that have served us so well across seven post-war
decades. As a result, those alliances have lost credibility with
growing numbers here who say they prefer that we stay at home
instead of supporting our allies overseas. In many of our allied
nations, similar doubts are taking root.

Why is it essential that we
support our allies? It's more than a matter of protecting our own
borders and preserving our national identity, essential as those
goals may be. It's also about protecting the collective human
values that have for so long sustained the United States and what
we rightly call the Free World - values such as freedom of
speech; universal respect for all without distinction as to race,
sex, language or religion; and a world open to free enterprise,
travel and trade. These are the shared values that we and our
allied nations believe in; the same values others scorn and deny
to those they rule.

Through seven stressful decades
since the end of World War II, these values and the moral
standards they embrace have been the foundation of an
international security framework, including NATO, which has
helped us avoid global war and provided its partners widespread
economic opportunity with free trade.

Unfortunately, that system and
its fundamental values are under siege today as most
participating countries have allowed our international security
structure to erode. But history, always eager to relive its past,
foretells what happens when our international alliances are taken
for granted and left to decay, despite the rise of increasingly
dangerous aggressors. Twice before, on the eve of World War I and
again in the dark days before World War II, we witnessed regional
instability that quickly led to global conflict - leaving tens of
millions dead and large swaths of the world in ruin.

Fast forward to today, and we can
see some of the same erosive forces at work: a weakening NATO and
growing conflict in regions such as Georgia, Crimea and Eastern
Ukraine. Perennial tensions and bloody conflict in the Middle
East are joined by gathering storm clouds in North Korea and the
South China Sea - and overhanging it all, international
terrorism. Calls for disengagement, isolation and shuttered trade
add to the sense that our world is coming undone.

Our nation is safer when freedom,
democracy and the rule of law are embraced around the world. In
places like Ukraine, there is a deep yearning for these values
and we must encourage that desire, not dismiss it. The notion
that it somehow makes us safer at home to sacrifice support for a
free Ukraine in exchange for a better relationship with Russia -
which continues to deny its unacceptable interference in our
presidential election - is wrong and naïve. It's inconsistent
with our ideals and leads other allies to doubt our resolve.
Putin only respects strength, which is one reason why I support
Senator John McCain's call for tougher sanctions against Russia
and Putin's inner circle.

Further encouraging this erosion,
our once-vigilant alliances have failed to deal with the most
urgent issues facing them, challenges such as the refugee crisis,
secure borders, cybersecurity and intelligence-sharing in the
face of terrorism. We need to fix those weaknesses, but in ways
that help our alliances evolve, not by throwing away the
underlying values that have held us together and kept the peace
for so many decades.
Rather than allowing history to
replay its sad lessons, now is the time for us to rediscover the
spirit of unity and social solidarity we need to restore the
functioning of our own democracies. We must also find in
ourselves once again the courage to stand up for our values
internationally - the values on which our shared system of
security was built and that have become too easy for us to take
for granted.

History will again show that it
takes courageous resolve to preserve the values-based alliances
that have kept us safe for seven decades. Without a shared
commitment to freedom, how can expect it to survive? That is why
we must reassure the Baltics and Ukraine - who live in the very
shadow of Russia - that the United States will be there for them
if trouble arises. Russian intimidation of our NATO allies or
other free nations cannot be tolerated

Instead of listening to the siren
song of false prophets, we must relearn to work together with
respect for opposing points of views in a search for the common
ground and a recommitment to shared values that will help us
together reaffirm our common humanity. We must strengthen our
alliances with renewed vigor and purpose by rededicating
ourselves to the values on which those bonds of trust are built.

It will not be easy in a time of
austerity and anger to push against a rising tide of opposition,
but as men and women of conscience, we must stand together and
have the moral courage to look into the heart of darkness and do
what is best for mankind.

The relentless repetition of
history teaches us this: If we find the courage and unity to
defend our values, we will succeed. And succeed we must.

John Kasichs latest op-ed - a call for America to strengthen our international alliances, to stand up to those who seek to divide us and to proudly champion the values that have paved the way for freedom around the world. Peter, we wanted to share with you Gov. John Kasich's latest op-ed - a call for America to strengthen our international alliances, to stand up to those who seek to divide us and to proudly champion the values that have paved the way for freedom around the world. Excerpt: "Fast forward to today, and we can see some of the same erosive forces at work: a weakening NATO and growing conflict in regions such as Georgia, Crimea and Eastern Ukraine. Perennial tensions and bloody conflict in the Middle East are joined by gathering storm clouds in North Korea and the South China Sea - and overhanging it all, international terrorism. Calls for disengagement, isolation and shuttered trade add to the sense that our world is coming undone. Our nation is safer when freedom, democracy and the rule of law are embraced around the world. In places like Ukraine, there is a deep yearning for these values and we must encourage that desire, not dismiss it. The notion that it somehow makes us safer at home to sacrifice support for a free Ukraine in exchange for a better relationship with Russia - which continues to deny its unacceptable interference in our presidential election - is wrong and naïve. It's inconsistent with our ideals and leads other allies to doubt our resolve. Putin only respects strength, which is one reason why I support Senator John McCain's call for tougher sanctions against Russia and Putin's inner circle." Read more from TIME and please take a moment to help us share it on Facebook and Twitter. Full Op-Ed: It is conventional wisdom that history repeats itself. But the world should hope - and pray - that this maxim is off-base when it comes to our global security. Security arrangements, like the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO), which through 71 years since the end of World War II have kept us safe from yet another global conflict, are quickly showing signs of coming undone. And if history is indeed repeating itself, we can't say we haven't been warned, and we must take steps now to change our current course. As governor of a state that is restoring its fiscal well-being by keeping pace with the demands and rewards of a global economy, I am increasingly concerned about the future of international alliances that has long ensured the United States and its partner nations of a stable world and the free flow of ideas and trade. These are not new concerns for me, having served nine terms in Congress, including 18 years on the House Armed Services Committee. From those perspectives I have feared this unraveling for some time, but my concerns are redoubled by recent threats to our alliances, both here and abroad. Growing tensions across the world, fed by intemperate voices at home, have caused many in this county to second-guess the international alliances and relationships that have served us so well across seven post-war decades. As a result, those alliances have lost credibility with growing numbers here who say they prefer that we stay at home instead of supporting our allies overseas. In many of our allied nations, similar doubts are taking root. Why is it essential that we support our allies? It's more than a matter of protecting our own borders and preserving our national identity, essential as those goals may be. It's also about protecting the collective human values that have for so long sustained the United States and what we rightly call the Free World - values such as freedom of speech; universal respect for all without distinction as to race, sex, language or religion; and a world open to free enterprise, travel and trade. These are the shared values that we and our allied nations believe in; the same values others scorn and deny to those they rule. Through seven stressful decades since the end of World War II, these values and the moral standards they embrace have been the foundation of an international security framework, including NATO, which has helped us avoid global war and provided its partners widespread economic opportunity with free trade. Unfortunately, that system and its fundamental values are under siege today as most participating countries have allowed our international security structure to erode. But history, always eager to relive its past, foretells what happens when our international alliances are taken for granted and left to decay, despite the rise of increasingly dangerous aggressors. Twice before, on the eve of World War I and again in the dark days before World War II, we witnessed regional instability that quickly led to global conflict - leaving tens of millions dead and large swaths of the world in ruin. Fast forward to today, and we can see some of the same erosive forces at work: a weakening NATO and growing conflict in regions such as Georgia, Crimea and Eastern Ukraine. Perennial tensions and bloody conflict in the Middle East are joined by gathering storm clouds in North Korea and the South China Sea - and overhanging it all, international terrorism. Calls for disengagement, isolation and shuttered trade add to the sense that our world is coming undone. Our nation is safer when freedom, democracy and the rule of law are embraced around the world. In places like Ukraine, there is a deep yearning for these values and we must encourage that desire, not dismiss it. The notion that it somehow makes us safer at home to sacrifice support for a free Ukraine in exchange for a better relationship with Russia - which continues to deny its unacceptable interference in our presidential election - is wrong and naïve. It's inconsistent with our ideals and leads other allies to doubt our resolve. Putin only respects strength, which is one reason why I support Senator John McCain's call for tougher sanctions against Russia and Putin's inner circle. Further encouraging this erosion, our once-vigilant alliances have failed to deal with the most urgent issues facing them, challenges such as the refugee crisis, secure borders, cybersecurity and intelligence-sharing in the face of terrorism. We need to fix those weaknesses, but in ways that help our alliances evolve, not by throwing away the underlying values that have held us together and kept the peace for so many decades. Rather than allowing history to replay its sad lessons, now is the time for us to rediscover the spirit of unity and social solidarity we need to restore the functioning of our own democracies. We must also find in ourselves once again the courage to stand up for our values internationally - the values on which our shared system of security was built and that have become too easy for us to take for granted. History will again show that it takes courageous resolve to preserve the values-based alliances that have kept us safe for seven decades. Without a shared commitment to freedom, how can expect it to survive? That is why we must reassure the Baltics and Ukraine - who live in the very shadow of Russia - that the United States will be there for them if trouble arises. Russian intimidation of our NATO allies or other free nations cannot be tolerated Instead of listening to the siren song of false prophets, we must relearn to work together with respect for opposing points of views in a search for the common ground and a recommitment to shared values that will help us together reaffirm our common humanity. We must strengthen our alliances with renewed vigor and purpose by rededicating ourselves to the values on which those bonds of trust are built. It will not be easy in a time of austerity and anger to push against a rising tide of opposition, but as men and women of conscience, we must stand together and have the moral courage to look into the heart of darkness and do what is best for mankind. The relentless repetition of history teaches us this: If we find the courage and unity to defend our values, we will succeed. And succeed we must. You can read the original op-ed on TIME (here). body:
About yesterday's press conference...
---
**It's up to us to prevent the most corrupt, conflict-ridden administration in history. Pitch in $3 or more to help Democrats hold Trump accountable.

** **[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c006/4067b0a/2325fea2c/7e78777d/3389095825/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VFgwTlZVMVJQVFY5R1NVVk1SRnR6YkhWblBXdGxlV052WkdWZEpTVT0iOiIifQ==/ "http://my.democrats.org/Help-Democrats-Fight-Back1?keycode=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170112_ew_nd&source=em_20170112_ew_nd")**
If there's one (and maybe only one) thing Donald Trump knows, it's that perception can very easily become reality.

He knows from personal experience: despite having filed for six business bankruptcies, Trump was able to brand himself as a "successful" businessman through a reality TV show that convinced millions of Americans he was.

And just yesterday, Trump held his first press conference since July. The goal was to address his conflicts of interest and how he would divest from his business interests before taking office. He even brought out an ethics lawyer (who works for him) and stacks of folders (which reporters were not allowed to examine) to make it look like he was taking this seriously!

Instead, we...

* Learned his sons -- who he would still be in regular contact with -- would be running his businesses while he was in office. This is not a blind trust or a divestment. This is him stepping away for a few years but still having a personal interest in his own bottom line.

* Heard Trump claim that "only reporters" cared about the release of his tax returns -- which would give us a pretty good idea of what potential conflicts, foreign or domestic, our president-elect might have. In fact, back in August, 74 percent of Americans -- including 62 percent of Republicans -- said he had a responsibility to release his returns.

* Witnessed the man who is about to be President compare the intelligence community to Nazi Germany and refuse to take questions from news organizations who published reports about him that he didn't like.

This was a preview of what's to come. We're going to have to speak out and fight back every single day to hold Trump and the GOP accountable. Pitch in $3 or whatever you can today to help us do it.

name: Katie McGinty
subject: FWD: help protect our air and water
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/979540774448267264/-9154384279281598464)
---
Friends,

Environmental protection is personal to me. It's animated my life's work. And it's at the core of the EPA's mission. But today, many of the public health safeguards that we've worked for so hard are under threat.

The nomination of Scott Pruitt -- a self-professed climate denier -- to lead the EPA represents a turn against decades of progress for clean air and water. If Pruitt follows his courtroom track record, he could undermine the Clean Power Plan, unravel the Clean Water Rule, and roll back air toxin standards that protect children.

**[Please join me today in taking a stand for our air and water and for tackling climate change. Tell your Senator to oppose the Pruitt nomination.](https://act.myngp.com/el/979540774448267264/-9154384279281598464)**

In Pennsylvania, we already have seven of the 25 most polluted cities in America. And we've seen that when we lead on the environment instead of drag our feet, it is good for jobs and the economy in addition to protecting the health of our kids. An EPA run by someone who has fought tirelessly against the environment and public health would be a huge set back that we can not afford. **[We deserve an Environmental Protection Agency that lives up to its name.](https://act.myngp.com/el/979540774448267264/-9154384279281598464)**

Thank you,

Katie McGinty

\---_Original message_ \---
From: Shaughnessy Naughton, info@314action.org
Subject line: help protect our air and water

Friends,

A reactionary President takes office vowing to dismantle the EPA. His EPA chief soon follows through on the agenda -- overseeing a 22% budget cut, dropping cases against polluters, and then resigns in the midst of an ethics investigation.

That's what happened the last time someone as conservative as Oklahoma Attorney General and avowed climate denier Scott Pruitt was tapped to the lead the EPA. We can't afford a supercharged version of the 1980s. Not at a time when the earth is slipping deeper into a climate crisis. **[Add your name to urge the Senate to reject Scott Pruitt's nomination.](https://act.myngp.com/el/979540774448267264/-9154384279281598464)**

Pruitt isn't just a champion of fossil fuels in court. He created a Super PAC to scoop up industry donations. And he was even was caught coordinating with energy lobbyists to attack clean air and water standards. He used his public office to defend the big polluters, and they showered him with hundreds of thousands of dollars in campaign contributions.

Now Pruitt could run Environmental Protection Agency. His confirmation hearings will start soon, and the fossil fuel interests that prop up Pruitt are already mobilizing. **[We must act now to draw a clear marker- the EPA should protect the environment, not the polluters.](https://act.myngp.com/el/979540774448267264/-9154384279281598464) **

After consulting with scientists and the general public, the EPA is leading the effort to reduce greenhouse gas emissions that worsen climate change. Yet Pruitt sued to void those regulations. It's no surprise that he's a climate denier. Pruitt's views on climate science are so extreme that they've even been denounced by members of his own party.

**The former head of the EPA in the George W. Bush Administration, Christine Todd Whitman, crystallized the bipartisan case against Pruitt:**_" I don't recall ever having seen an appointment of someone who is so disdainful of the agency and the science behind what the agency does."_

As Administrator of the EPA, Pruitt could roll back a decade's worth of progress, and undermine America's leadership on climate change. His zeal for deregulation threatens the lives of people who are exposed to toxins in the air and water.

The confirmation battle ahead won't be easy. The same climate deniers that spent decades and untold millions attacking climate science are doing all they can to push through the Pruitt nomination. But they can't change the facts -- the science is on our side, and so are the American people, who overwhelmingly support climate action.

**[Act today to send a clear message to the Senate: don't roll back decades of progress. Reject Scott Pruitt.](https://act.myngp.com/el/979540774448267264/-9154384279281598464)**

Thank you for all you do,

Shaughnessy Naughton

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Still climbing that mountain
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
This must finally be the year that we reach the summit and fully fund K-12 education. [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTAyMTg3NzES1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTA4MTAwMjcxNQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
| Friend, Many Washingtonians know the story of Jim Whittaker -- the first American to summit Mount Everest. You see, Washingtonians like Jim don't like taking the easy way out -- we do hard things, we climb tall mountains. That's who we are and that's what we do. So yesterday, during my second inaugural address, I talked about another summit we must reach, together: fully funding K-12 education once and for all. It's what our founders wanted, it's the right thing to do for our children, and it's what we must finally accomplish this year. We must choose to build on the enduring foundation of Washington, its most promising resource -- the intellectual light of our children. Our founders understood this, and so must we. That's why I've proposed a serious plan to get us there, by making hard choices about spending and asking the wealthiest among us to pay their fair share, harnessing the great potential of our state, our education professionals, parents, and kids. [**If you 're with me on this climb, join me on Facebook and share your commitment to fight for a Washington state that fully funds K-12 education.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MTAyMTg3NzES1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTA4MTAwMjcxNQS2&mt=1&rt=0) [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTAyMTg3NzES1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTA4MTAwMjcxNQS2&mt=1&rt=0) There are multiple routes we could take. I have proposed one that gets us there this year, a route based on what I've seen work as I've visited schools around the state from Spokane to Yakima Valley to Tacoma. And I also know that fully funding education is only part of the commitment we must make to our kids, especially as we're days away from the start of the Trump Administration. We will not turn our back on the progress we have made. Our commitment to equal rights and human dignity will not be diminished. We will fight for our values. And we will fight for the education system we know our kids deserve. So let's start this session with a shared commitment to all and excuses to none. A recognition that the best thing we can do in service to our children and state this year is to fully fund the education system they deserve. [**Join me right now and share you support on Facebook for this bold vision.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTAyMTg3NzES1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTA4MTAwMjcxNQS2&mt=1&rt=0) My inauguration is over. The legislative session has begun. Let's get to work finishing the climb, so this year we finally reach that summit together -- I bet Jim would be proud, and so will our kids. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [**SHARE YOUR SUPPORT »**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTAyMTg3NzES1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTA4MTAwMjcxNQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

name: Kate Black, EMILY's List
subject: UPDATE: Senate Republicans start to repeal Obamacare
body:
| [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7087/24c69fd9/fdbc59eb/18eeb4bb/4058442694/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE112N_digital_email_20170112_ACArepeal_n-all&source=WQEFE112N_digital_email_20170112_ACArepeal_n-all")
---

Peter,

The Senate just voted in the dead of night to start repeal of the Affordable Care Act.

This could be catastrophic for the millions of women and families across America who will lose coverage if the bill makes it through the House and is signed by President-elect Trump.

**[Donate $3 now to help elect more pro-choice Democratic women who will fight back against Republican attempts to repeal Obamacare.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7087/24c69fd9/fdbc59eb/18eeb4b4/4058442694/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170112_email_ACArepeal?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+now+to+help+elect+more+prochoic&utm_campaign=WQEFE112N_digital_email_20170112_ACArepeal_n-all&source=WQEFE112N_digital_email_20170112_ACArepeal_n-all")**

The Affordable Care Act makes sure women have access to birth control and that no one can be turned down for insurance coverage because of a pre-existing condition, and it bars insurance companies from charging women more for coverage than men.

Despite voting over 60 times in six years to repeal the health care law, Republicans don't even have a plan to replace it.

We have to elect more pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women's health care and stop Republicans from rolling back progress.

**Donate $3 to help our women gear up for 2018:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Protect-The-ACA](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7087/24c69fd9/fdbc59eb/18eeb4b5/4058442694/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170112_email_ACArepeal?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgProtectTheACA&utm_campaign=WQEFE112N_digital_email_20170112_ACArepeal_n-all&source=WQEFE112N_digital_email_20170112_ACArepeal_n-all")**

Thanks,

Kate Black
